description: Patch: prevent runaway `git add -f .devflow/` on gitignored projects + Pi platform workflow-state injection + Pi pi-subagents project isolation.
changelog:
**Bug Fixes:**
- fix(scripts): `add_session.py` and `task.py archive` no longer print a generic `git add .devflow && git commit` fallback when the repo's `.gitignore` excludes `.devflow/`. They now stage only specific DevFlow-owned paths (journal, index.md, active task dir, archive subtree) and auto-retry with `git add -f -- <specific-paths>` only on `ignored by` stderr. The warning text explicitly states `Do NOT use \`git add -f .devflow/\``, naming `.devflow/.backup-*`, `.devflow/worktrees/`, `.devflow/.template-hashes.json`, `.devflow/.runtime/`, `.devflow/.cache/` as the subpaths users should ignore individually instead. Helper centralized in `templates/devflow/scripts/common/safe_commit.py`.
- fix(pi): Pi extension now injects `<workflow-state>` breadcrumb on every `input` and `before_agent_start` event, plus a `<session-overview>` block sourced from `.devflow/scripts/get_context.py`. The `subagent` tool registration carries a `promptSnippet` with the `Active task: <path>` dispatch protocol. Pi sessions previously skipped the `task.py create → brainstorm → implement → check` flow because the AI saw no workflow guidance after session start. Closes #249.
- fix(pi): Project-level `packages["npm:pi-subagents"]` override added to `.pi/settings.json` so a globally-installed `npm:pi-subagents` cannot inject `extensions / skills / prompts / themes` into the current DevFlow project. `scrubPiSettings` reverses the override on `devflow uninstall`. Closes #246.
notes: Patch on top of 0.5.9. Run `devflow update` (no `--migrate` needed). All three fixes auto-apply on next session.
